name: Tempeh Paprikas with Tahini
recipeInstructions:
1. Pre-heat oven to 300°F.
2. Heat oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
3. Saute onions and paprika until golden brown.
4. Add 1 1/2 cups of vegetable stock and miso to the skillet. Bring to a boil.
5. Add the tempeh pieces to the skillet, reduce heat to low, and simmer for 35 to 40 minutes.
6. In a separate bowl, blend soy sauce, cider vinegar, and tahini with the remaining vegetable stock until smooth.
7. Stir the blended mixture into the skillet, allowing it to thicken without boiling.
Note: This recipe serves as closely as possible to the original while ensuring clarity and consistency in ingredients and instructions.
